# Regression: a failing sesam command must print its error to stderr and leave
# stdout completely untouched.
#
# When sesam runs as the git filter `process` (sesam-filter), git reads
# pkt-lines from sesam's stdout; as the sesam-diff `textconv` it reads the
# decrypted content from stdout; as the sesam-merge driver it likewise drives
# stdout. A stray "✘ ..." error line on stdout corrupts that stream and git
# aborts with `fatal: protocol error: bad line length character: ✘`.
#
# `sesam smudge` with no identity fails before reading git's request stream,
# which is the exact path that produced the corruption.

env SESAM_ID=
env SESAM_IDENTITY=
! exec sesam smudge
stderr 'need at least one identity'

# Nothing at all may reach stdout on the error path.
! stdout .
